seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI agents, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest as poor chunk relevance during tool calls and memory retrieval, leading to hallucinated responses and degraded agent performance. BeeAI's memory system and tool integration amplify these problems when vector stores return irrelevant context, causing agents to reason over noisy data. This deep-dive covers detection patterns specific to BeeAI's event system and APIs.

howItManifests.scenario: Long-context tool calls with irrelevant chunks
howItManifests.symptom: BeeAI's `ToolContext` returns chunks with cosine similarity < 0.7
howItManifests.impact: Agent generates incorrect tool parameters, causing API failures

howItManifests.symptom: BeeAI `MemoryRetriever` scores drop below 0.6 for session history
howItManifests.impact: Agent loses conversation context, repeats questions

howItManifests.scenario: Hybrid search failing on semantic queries
howItManifests.symptom: BeeAI `VectorStore.query()` returns empty or mismatched results
howItManifests.impact: Fallback to parametric generation creates hallucinations

howItManifests.scenario: Chunk size mismatch with embedding model
howItManifests.symptom: BeeAI `DocumentChunker` produces chunks > 512 tokens for granite embeddings

detection.name: Retrieval Score Monitoring
detection.description: Capture BeeAI VectorStore query events and alert on average similarity < 0.75
detection.code:
from beeai import Agent
from vex_sdk import VexListener

class RetrievalQualityListener(VexListener):
    def on_retrieval_complete(self, event):
        scores = [hit.score for hit in event.retrieval_results]
        avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
        if avg_score < 0.75:
            self.vex.capture_metric('rag_retrieval_score', avg_score)
            self.vex.flag_issue('LOW_RETRIEVAL_QUALITY', f'Avg score: {avg_score}')

agent = Agent()
agent.add_event_listener(RetrievalQualityListener())

detection.name: Chunk Relevance Validator
detection.description: Validate BeeAI ToolContext chunks contain query keywords using cosine similarity
detection.code:
from beeai.tools import ToolContext
from vex_sdk import VexListener
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity

class ChunkValidator(VexListener):
    def on_tool_context_ready(self, event: ToolContext):
        query_emb = event.query_embedding
        chunk_embs = [chunk.embedding for chunk in event.chunks]
        similarities = cosine_similarity([query_emb], chunk_embs)[0]
        
        low_relevance_chunks = sum(s < 0.7 for s in similarities)
        if low_relevance_chunks / len(event.chunks) > 0.3:
            self.vex.flag_issue('IRRELEVANT_CHUNKS', 
                f'{low_relevance_chunks}/{len(event.chunks)} chunks < 0.7 similarity')

detection.name: Memory Retrieval Drift Detector
detection.description: Monitor BeeAI MemoryRetriever score decay over conversation turns

fixes.name: Dynamic Chunk Size Optimization
fixes.description: Adjust BeeAI DocumentChunker size based on embedding model limits
fixes.code:
from beeai.document import DocumentChunker
from beeai.embeddings import GraniteEmbedding

chunker = DocumentChunker(
    chunk_size=384,  # Optimized for granite-3b
    chunk_overlap=64,
    embedding_model=GraniteEmbedding(model='ibm/granite-3b')
)

# Register with agent
agent.document_processor = chunker

# Vex validation
vex.listener.on_chunking_complete(lambda event: 
    vex.capture_metric('chunk_token_count', 
        sum(len(c.text.split()) for c in event.chunks)))

fixes.name: Hybrid Search with Reranking
fixes.description: Combine BeeAI keyword + vector search with cross-encoder reranking
fixes.code:
from beeai.retrievers import HybridRetriever
from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder

reranker = CrossEncoder('cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2')

class RerankedHybridRetriever(HybridRetriever):
    def retrieve(self, query, k=5):
        # Get top 20 candidates
        candidates = super().retrieve(query, k=20)
        
        # Rerank
        pairs = [[query, doc.text] for doc in candidates]
        scores = reranker.predict(pairs)
        
        # Return top k
        ranked = sorted(zip(candidates, scores), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
        return [doc for doc, score in ranked[:k]]

agent.retriever = RerankedHybridRetriever()

fixes.name: Query Rewrite for Better Retrieval
fixes.description: Automatically rewrite vague queries using BeeAI's LLM before retrieval

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly with BeeAI's event system as a listener, capturing retrieval scores, chunk relevance, and memory drift in real-time. It automatically baselines healthy retrieval patterns from your production traffic and alerts when scores deviate beyond 2σ. Remediation workflows trigger dynamic retriever reconfiguration, chunker optimization, and query rewriting without agent restarts.

Vex's BeeAI integration provides production-grade observability with one-line setup. It captures 100% of retrieval events, computes sliding window baselines, and executes auto-remediation through BeeAI's configuration API. Teams see retrieval quality dashboards and receive Slack alerts with one-click fixes.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from beeai import Agent

# One-line Vex + BeeAI integration
vex = Vex(api_key='vex-xxxxx')
agent = Agent()

@vex.listener()
class BeeAIRAGMonitor:
    def on_retrieval(self, event):
        # Auto-baseline retrieval quality
        vex.baseline('rag_avg_score', event.avg_score)
        
        # Auto-remediate low quality
        if event.avg_score < vex.baseline('rag_avg_score') - 2 * vex.std('rag_avg_score'):
            agent.retriever = OptimizedRetriever(agent.retriever)
            vex.notify('Auto-upgraded retriever due to quality drift')
        
# Deploy
agent.add_event_listener(vex.listener())
agent.run()

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install beeai-framework vex-sdk` and enable RAG optimization in 3 lines of code.
